channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Wisconsin’s governor creates new violence-prevention office in wake of school shooting
item.description: Wisconsin’s Democratic governor on Tuesday created a new office dedicated to preventing gun violence, a month after a school shooting not far from the state Capitol and an idea that drew immediate opposition from Republicans who said it was misguided.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden in late push to boost Indo-Pacific ties sends three pacts to Congress
item.description: The Biden administration is seeking to shore up ties with countries in the Indo-Pacific in its waning days in office by sending to Congress three key agreements that would cement relations with critical allies and partners in the region, which is increasingly dominated by China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOJ racing the clock to enshrine 'woke' policing rules, lawyer says, as judge hears Breonna Taylor reform case
item.description: A marathon seven-hour Louisville police reform consent decree hearing did not produce the results the Biden DOJ appeared to hope for, according to one attorney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top DC prosecutor: Trump pardons couldn't erase impact of Capitol riot convictions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pardoning rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol four years ago can't erase the truth about what happened that day, the top federal prosecutor for Washington, D.C., said Tuesday as he prepares to leave office. “There is no undoing these prosecutions,” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP tax leaders: US small businesses could pay more tax than small businesses do in China if Trump cuts expire
item.description: Republican members of the chief House committee on taxes said if Trump's tax cuts expire small businesses could begin paying a higher tax rate than small businesses do in communist China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gold Star families devastated by Biden's botched Afghanistan withdrawal endorse Hegseth for SecDef
item.description: Gold Star families who lost loved ones during the Biden administration's botched withdrawal from Afghanistan endorsed Pete Hegseth to be the next secretary of Defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY plan would make community college free: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to make community college free for adults pursuing in-demand careers.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Top 5 moments from Pete Hegseth's Senate confirmation hearing
item.description: Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump's choice to head up the Pentagon, faced a Senate confirmation grilling from the Armed Services Committee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two House Dems join GOP to ban biological males from girls' school sports
item.description: The House of Representatives has passed legislation that would restrict girls' school sports to only those who were born female, excluding transgender kids.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama will skip Trump inauguration, but ex-Presidents Obama, Clinton and Bush will be there
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Former first lady Michelle Obama will skip the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump, the second time in two weeks that she is not attending a gathering of former U.S. leaders and their spouses, but former Presidents Barack Obama, George W.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he will create an 'External Revenue Service' agency to collect tariff income
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday announced plans to create a new agency called the External Revenue Service to collect tariffs and other revenues from foreign nations.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Honoring Trump: Speaker Johnson says flags to fly full-staff at US Capitol during presidential inauguration
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson announced on Tuesday that flags at U.S. Capitol will be raised to full-staff next Monday, Jan. 20, to mark President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration, during the official mourning period for former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court weighs appeal of ex-Chicago alderman’s corruption conviction
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday signaled it may send a Chicago political scion’s appeal of his conviction for lying to regulators back to a lower court to flesh out the difference between false and misleading statements.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 ways the debt limit fight could play out under Trump
item.description: President-elect Trump and congressional Republicans are plotting how to address the nation’s debt limit this year. The debt ceiling, which caps how much money the Treasury can owe to pay the country’s bills, was reestablished earlier this month after being suspended for the past year and half.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson backs conditions on California fire aid
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is backing placing conditions on disaster assistance for California in the wake of the destructive wildfires blazing around the Los Angeles area, a position already drawing opposition from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: What to know from Pete Hegseth’s fiery confirmation hearing
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for Defense Secretary is facing senators for the first time after weeks of questions from Democrats and praise from Republicans about his “unconventional” resume.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How Trump's Greenland demands could impact Ozempic costs
item.description: President-elect Trump's threat to tariff Denmark if it resists his acquisition plans for the island territory of Greenland could disrupt one export that is wildly popular in America: Ozempic.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Schools brace for clash with immigration officials ahead of Trump term
item.description: K-12 schools across the U.S. are gearing up for potential efforts to shield their students as the incoming Trump administration sets its sights on deporting millions of people.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican infighting flares after Burrows elected speaker
item.description: The Texas House kicked off the 2025 session by electing an establishment Republican to lead the chamber, drawing criticism from within the GOP.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By James Barragán, Jasper Scherer And Pooja Salhotra, KPRC 2

item.title: Minnesota House DFLers boycott opening session; Republicans elect their own speaker
item.description: DFLers on Tuesday made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo as the parties fight over control. House Republicans pushed ahead anyway in the half-empty chamber. It’s all likely headed to the courts.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Meet the 9 candidates in an election to decide control of the Minnesota Senate
item.description: The recent death of Minneapolis state Sen. Kari Dziedzic left a 33-33 tie in the Minnesota Senate. A primary for her seat set for Tuesday features seven DFLers and two Republicans. The primary winners from each party will face each other in a Jan. 28 election.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Incoming Trump team questions civil servants at National Security Council about their loyalty
item.description: Incoming senior Trump officials have begun questioning career civil servants serving on the White House National Security Council about who they voted for, their political contributions in the 2024 election and whether they've made social media posts that could be considered incriminating by the Tru
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: CA firefighters face impersonators, staffing shortages, low pay
item.description: LA County Sheriff Robert Luna said he encountered a man dressed as a firefighter burglarizing a home.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about Biden and his son Hunter gets 6 years in prison
item.description: A former FBI informant who fabricated a story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes that became central to Republicans’ impeachment effort was sentenced Wednesday to six years in prison.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
